concept: Narrow / Of Small Width / Limited in Breadth
description: Of small width in comparison to length; limited in extent from side to side. 'Hieru' names the quality of narrowness—constrained breadth, focused passage.
sound_symbolism: The word draws inward. 'Hi' begins with contained breath, width limiting. The vowel 'e' passes through carefully. Then 'ru' continues in constrained flow, narrowness held.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'hieru ruela' (narrow path). Opposite of 'losha' (wide). Can describe physical space or metaphorical focus.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Narrow focus enables depth—concentration that excludes distraction penetrates further.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Narrow paths required attention—single-file walking through forest demanded presence.
